lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Class, who do you admire the most? Yes, Yaming?

lines.speaker: yaming
lines.text: I really admire Yuan Longping. It was great of him to develop a new kind of rice and help reduce hunger.

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: That's great! Yuan Longping also trained many people to do research on farming in China and other parts of the world. Who else inspires you? Yes, Peter?

lines.speaker: peter
lines.text: I admire Michael Faraday very much. It was amazing that he did scientific research in physics and chemistry even though he didn't receive a formal education.

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Yes, Michael Faraday dropped out of school at an early age, but he found every chance to study and became a great scientist. Thanks!

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Who do your parents or grandparents admire? Do you know, Fu Xing?

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: Yes, Ms Gao. My father loves history. He admires Sima Qian.

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Ah yes, the author of the book Shiji (Records of the Historian). He also played a big part in reviewing the Chinese calendar at that time.

lines.speaker: fu_xing
lines.text: Oh, I didn't know that. But I know he was the kind of person who never gave up.

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Right. It's always important to keep going in life. What about you, Emma?

lines.speaker: emma
lines.text: My grandma told me about Marie Curie, a great scientist. She made many important discoveries in physics and chemistry.

lines.speaker: ms_gao
lines.text: Yes. I admire Madame Curie too! It was difficult for women to do that kind of work back in those days. And she won two Nobel Prizes!

lines.speaker: gina
lines.text: Dad, do you have a second? We need to do a survey of our family members about their most admired people. Can you help me, please?

lines.speaker: dad
lines.text: Sure. Go ahead.

lines.speaker: gina
lines.text: OK, Dad. Who do you admire the most?

lines.speaker: dad
lines.text: For me, it has to be Isambard Brunel, a famous English engineer. He built the first British railways and many other important things such as bridges, ships, and tunnels. All these helped the UK develop into a modern nation.

lines.speaker: gina
lines.text: Oh, I see. How about you, Mum?

lines.speaker: mum
lines.text: Well, I am really inspired by Florence Nightingale. She created modern nursing. It was wonderful of her to help us understand the need for cleaner hospitals to stop the spread of diseases.

lines.speaker: gina
lines.text: That's nice. What about you, Steve?

lines.speaker: steve
lines.text: Isaac Newton, of course. It was so important that he developed modern physics! I also admire Alan Turing. You know the famous Turing test? It's so cool!

lines.speaker: gina
lines.text: Yes, I agree. He was a pioneer of computer science. Thanks, Steve.
